124.reason: [Error: Recursive call without base case; BFS should be iterative using a queue, not recursive. This will cause infinite recursion and stack overflow.]

18.code: void DFS(struct ver  G[ ], int v);
18.reason: Missing forward declaration for function 'BFS', which is called in 'travelBFS'. This causes a compilation error due to implicit function declaration in C99 and later.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing forward declaration for function 'BFS', which is called in 'travelBFS'. This causes a compilation error due to implicit function declaration in C99 and later.

118.code: for(p=G[v].link; p!=NULL; p=p->next ) //访问该顶点的每个邻接顶点
118.reason: Logical error: 'p->next' does not update the pointer 'p', causing an infinite loop. It should be 'p=p->next'.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Logical error: 'p->next' does not update the pointer 'p', causing an infinite loop. It should be 'p=p->next'.

17.code: void  travelBFS(struct ver  G[ ], int n);
17.reason: Duplicate declaration of travelBFS. More importantly, the BFS function is called within travelBFS but lacks a forward declaration before main, causing an implicit declaration compilation error in modern C standards.

110.code: void BFS(struct ver  G[ ], int v)
110.reason: [Recursive BFS implementation is incorrect and leads to infinite recursion or stack overflow. BFS should be iterative using a queue, but this function calls itself recursively without proper base case or queue management. Also, it processes only one neighbor per call and misuses the global queue variables.]
